title: crepidomanes minutum subsp. proliferum
intro: Crepidomanes minutum subsp. proliferum, also known as the proliferous forked spleenwort, is a rare species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. As a medicinal plant, it is traditionally used for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids, tannins, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Crepidomanes minutum subsp. proliferum include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral and antibacterial activities. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
